description: Eval set for the the agency UI design skill. Tests that the skill triggers on design tasks, applies the locked tokens, rejects banned patterns, and composes correctly with guided-tour.

evals.prompt: Create a new KPI card component that shows total revenue with a variance indicator. It should feel premium.
expectations: Uses --ne-bg-base or #FBFBFA for card background, not bg-white
Uses rounded-xl or smaller, never rounded-2xl or rounded-3xl
Uses JetBrains Mono (or font-mono) for the numeric value
Uses DM Sans for the label
Uses --ne-kpi-value or clamp() responsive sizing for the number
Uppercase label is tracked wide (tracking-wider or letter-spacing)
Variance indicator uses semantic token (--ne-success or --ne-critical) based on sign
Applies the signature hover curve (transition-all duration-300 hover:shadow-lg hover:-translate-y-0.5) OR uses the ne-card-interactive class
Does NOT use emoji, backdrop-blur, or bg-gradient-to-*
Card border is hairline (border-[var(--ne-border-hairline)] or #EAEAEA)

evals.prompt:
Review this component for design quality:

Revenue 🎉

R1,151,700
expectations: Flags rounded-2xl as banned (should cap at rounded-xl)
Flags backdrop-blur-md as glassmorphism ban violation
Flags bg-white/30 as translucent white ban violation
Flags shadow-2xl as too blurry for cards
Flags emoji in heading as banned
Flags text-pink-500 as unsemantic color (pink is not a the agency semantic slot)
Proposes corrected version using --ne-bg-base, rounded-xl, two-layer shadow, neutral card, semantic variance color only if the number is carrying a variance signal

evals.prompt: I want the hero KPI cards at the top of my dashboard to feel distinctly the agency — apply the silver signature.
expectations: Applies Mode A (hairline metallic border) via ne-silver-edge class or ne-card-premium combination
Uses the locked silver gradient (#D4D7DC → #B8BCC2 → #D4D7DC) not a flat gray
Uses mask-composite technique for the 1px gradient border
Does NOT apply silver as a background fill
Does NOT animate the silver (no shimmer, no gradient-flow)
Pairs with Mode C hover ring (--ne-silver-ring at 40% opacity)
Does NOT use on non-premium surfaces (buttons, inputs, list rows)

evals.prompt: Fix the bug in the Supabase RPC get_dashboard_canonical_metrics where the trip count is off by one
Query the fleet_events table to show me all LOAD_COMPLETE events from yesterday
I'm starting a new client project called Acme Logistics. Their brand color is orange #EA580C. Set up the design tokens.
expectations: Copies tokens.css from the skill's assets/ into the project
Replaces {{primary_color}} with #EA580C
Replaces {{primary_color_foreground}} with an accessible contrast color (white or near-black)
Does NOT change the locked neutrals (#FBFBFA / #F7F6F3 / #EAEAEA / #E8E7E3)
Does NOT change the locked semantic palette (#B42318 / #B54708 / #067647 / #175CD3)
Does NOT change the locked silver values
Imports DM Sans + JetBrains Mono via @fontsource or Google Fonts
Does NOT import Inter, Roboto, or system-ui as primary

evals.prompt: Audit the current project's UI for design compliance issues
expectations: Runs scripts/audit-forbidden-patterns.sh on the target project root
Runs scripts/audit-colors.sh on the target project root
Runs scripts/audit-hover-consistency.sh on the target project root
Reports violations as file:line references with explanation
Produces a markdown table of findings ordered by severity
Does NOT modify files — audit is read-only

evals.prompt: Make the status badges more colorful — add pastel backgrounds so they stand out
expectations: Rejects the request to use pastel backgrounds
Explains the color discipline rule (semantic-only, pastels banned)
Proposes alternative: use the locked --ne-critical-bg / --ne-warning-bg / --ne-success-bg tokens (which are tuned neutrals, not pastels)
Offers the text-only neutral variant for non-state badges
Does NOT add emoji or decorative icons to badges

evals.prompt: Build a dashboard header with a 'Take a Tour' button that matches the client's brand color
expectations: References guided-tour skill for tour button implementation (does not reimplement it)
Uses {{primary_color}} parameter for the tour button background
Uses {{primary_color_foreground}} for the tour button text
Does NOT override guided-tour's z-index (10000/10001)
Does NOT add backdrop-filter anywhere in the header
Header itself uses neutral --ne-bg-base, not a gradient or colored background

evals.prompt: Deploy the edge function daily-fleet-report to supabase

success_criteria.notes: Should trigger on UI/design/theming tasks. Should NOT trigger on backend, data, or infrastructure tasks even when the codebase contains both.
